source_paper_title: Higher-Order Expansion and Bartlett Correctability of Distributionally Robust Optimization
context:
Section 8 "Generalization to Multidimensional Settings", page 16 (PDF page 16/51).

We discuss a potential way to generalize our results to the multidimensional case, leaving the full investigation to future work. The goal is to construct a confidence region for a multidimensional von Mises functional.
title: Higher-order Bartlett-corrected DRO confidence regions for multidimensional von Mises functionals
background_latex:
- Let \(X_1,\dots,X_n\) be i.i.d. samples from an unknown distribution \(P_0\) on a measurable space.
- Let \(\hat P:=\frac{1}{n}\sum_{i=1}^n \delta_{X_i}\) be the empirical distribution.
- Let \(\psi\) be a (von Mises differentiable) statistical functional with values in \(\mathbb R^d\), i.e., \(\psi(P)\in\mathbb R^d\) for a distribution \(P\).
- Let \(\phi:\mathbb R_+\to\mathbb R\) be a convex divergence generator and define the \(\phi\)-divergence \(D_\phi(P\|Q):=\int \phi\!\left(\frac{dP}{dQ}\right)\,dQ\) when \(P\ll Q\).
- For a radius \(\eta>0\), define the divergence ball around \(\hat P\) by \(\mathcal U_\eta:=\{P: D_\phi(P\|\hat P)\le \eta\}\).
- Define the DRO-induced confidence region (image of the ball under \(\psi\)) by
\[A_\eta := \{\psi(P): P\in \mathcal U_\eta\} \subseteq \mathbb R^d.\]
- Let \(\alpha\in(0,1)\) be the desired miscoverage level, and let \(n\to\infty\) denote the sample size asymptotic regime.
problem_definition_latex:
Determine a data-dependent (or asymptotically explicit) choice of radius \(\eta=\eta(n,\alpha)\) such that the DRO confidence region \(A_\eta\) achieves higher-order coverage accuracy
\[\mathbb P\big(\psi(P_0)\in A_{\eta(n,\alpha)}\big) \,=\, 1-\alpha + O(n^{-3/2}),\]
for multidimensional functionals \(\psi(P)\in\mathbb R^d\), and provide a rigorous asymptotic theory (e.g., a higher-order expansion and the needed Edgeworth-type validity conditions) supporting this guarantee.
latex:
- Let \(X_1,\dots,X_n\) be i.i.d. samples from an unknown distribution \(P_0\) on a measurable space.
- Let \(\hat P:=\frac{1}{n}\sum_{i=1}^n \delta_{X_i}\) be the empirical distribution.
- Let \(\psi\) be a (von Mises differentiable) statistical functional with values in \(\mathbb R^d\), i.e., \(\psi(P)\in\mathbb R^d\) for a distribution \(P\).
- Let \(\phi:\mathbb R_+\to\mathbb R\) be a convex divergence generator and define the \(\phi\)-divergence \(D_\phi(P\|Q):=\int \phi\!\left(\frac{dP}{dQ}\right)\,dQ\) when \(P\ll Q\).
- For a radius \(\eta>0\), define the divergence ball around \(\hat P\) by \(\mathcal U_\eta:=\{P: D_\phi(P\|\hat P)\le \eta\}\).
- Define the DRO-induced confidence region (image of the ball under \(\psi\)) by
\[A_\eta := \{\psi(P): P\in \mathcal U_\eta\} \subseteq \mathbb R^d.\]
- Let \(\alpha\in(0,1)\) be the desired miscoverage level, and let \(n\to\infty\) denote the sample size asymptotic regime.

Determine a data-dependent (or asymptotically explicit) choice of radius \(\eta=\eta(n,\alpha)\) such that the DRO confidence region \(A_\eta\) achieves higher-order coverage accuracy
\[\mathbb P\big(\psi(P_0)\in A_{\eta(n,\alpha)}\big) \,=\, 1-\alpha + O(n^{-3/2}),\]
for multidimensional functionals \(\psi(P)\in\mathbb R^d\), and provide a rigorous asymptotic theory (e.g., a higher-order expansion and the needed Edgeworth-type validity conditions) supporting this guarantee.
